verdict.text: Santos scores Caution. Brazil's largest port city has a significant crime profile connected to port-related drug trafficking and gang activity. The beachfront area is the tourist draw; the interior of the city and the port district carry higher risk.

reconciliation.text: The beachfront (Orla) is generally safer, though phone-snatching and beach theft occur. The Centro has robbery risk, especially at night. Some neighbourhoods associated with trafficking are off-limits to casual visitors.

If visiting for beaches, stick to the Orla and Gonzaga neighbourhoods. Do not venture into the port area or Centro without purpose. Santos is often a day-trip from São Paulo; staying overnight adds risk vs. daytime-only visits.

SSP-SP official crime statistics for Santos region — 2026:
HOMICÍDIO DOLOSO (2): 970 (2026); TENTATIVA DE HOMICÍDIO: 1,631 (2026); LESÃO CORPORAL SEGUIDA DE MORTE: 40 (2026); LESÃO CORPORAL DOLOSA: 73,747 (2026); LESÃO CORPORAL CULPOSA - OUTRAS: 1,426 (2026); LATROCÍNIO: 38 (2026); TOTAL DE ESTUPRO (4): 6,500 (2026); ESTUPRO: 1,514 (2026); ESTUPRO DE VULNERÁVEL: 4,986 (2026); TOTAL DE ROUBO - OUTROS (1): 60,374 (2026); ROUBO - OUTROS: 59,314 (2026); ROUBO DE VEÍCULO: 7,310 (2026); ROUBO DE CARGA: 1,060 (2026); FURTO - OUTROS: 221,268 (2026); FURTO DE VEÍCULO: 33,257 (2026).
reconciliation.source_name: SSP-SP — Secretaria da Segurança Pública do Estado de São Paulo (Santos)

reconciliation.text: World Bank / UNODC — national intentional homicide rate for Brazil: 2023: 19.3/100k; 2022: 20.1/100k; 2021: 20.5/100k; 2020: 21.8/100k. Note: this is a country-level rate, not city-specific. Source: World Bank Open Data (VC.IHR.PSRC.P5), compiled from UNODC Crime Trends Survey.
